entries.title: Frodo And Sam Travel Alone Toward Mordor

context.label: Frodo and Sam before Gollum guides them

content.fact: After leaving the Fellowship, Frodo and Sam continue toward Mordor alone until Gollum becomes their guide.
content.injection: In the early Two Towers Frodo/Sam route, keep Frodo and Sam isolated, burdened, and physically uncertain on the road toward Mordor.
constraints: Their secrecy and vulnerability increase because no larger company protects them.
antiLore: Do not include Aragorn, Legolas, Gimli, Merry, or Pippin traveling with them during this route unless the story diverges.

entries.title: Merry And Pippin Are Taken By Orcs

context.label: Merry and Pippin captivity before Fangorn

content.fact: Merry and Pippin are carried by Orcs after the Fellowship breaks, with rival Orc bands under pressure to deliver captives.
content.injection: During the early Two Towers arc, Merry and Pippin should be captives among Orcs until their escape near Fangorn.
constraints: Their captors are dangerous but internally divided by allegiance, discipline, and competing orders.
antiLore: Do not have Merry and Pippin safely with Aragorn during this span.

entries.title: Ugluk Uses Discipline While Grishnakh Uses Suspicion

context.label: Ugluk Uses Discipline While Grishnakh Uses Suspicion

content.fact: Ugluk enforces Isengard discipline while Grishnakh probes suspiciously for hidden value.
content.injection: Keep Ugluk and Grishnakh behavior distinct: one controls the march, the other seeks opportunity.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Aragorn Leads Before Kingship Is Settled

context.label: Aragorn leadership during The Two Towers

content.fact: Aragorn leads through skill, judgment, lineage, and personal authority before his kingship is publicly settled.
content.injection: In The Two Towers, portray Aragorn as a leader with deep authority and hidden royal weight, but not yet as an enthroned king whose claim is universally settled.
constraints: Rohan may respect him through deeds and bearing without fully centering Gondor's kingship politics.
antiLore: Do not make Aragorn politically king of Gondor during The Two Towers.

entries.title: The Three Hunters Pursue Merry And Pippin

context.label: Three Hunters pursuit before Gandalf returns

content.fact: Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli pursue the Orcs to rescue Merry and Pippin, reading signs and driving themselves past ordinary endurance.
content.injection: Before meeting Gandalf the White, Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli should be focused on pursuing the Orcs and rescuing Merry and Pippin.
constraints: Aragorn leads through tracking skill and duty rather than certainty.
antiLore: Do not send the Three Hunters directly to Helm's Deep without the Fangorn/Gandalf transition unless the story diverges.

entries.title: Eomer’s Exile Tests Loyalty To Rohan

context.label: Eomer’s Exile Tests Loyalty To Rohan

content.fact: Eomer’s exile shows loyalty to Rohan in conflict with corrupted court authority under Grima.
content.injection: Eomer should not read as rebel against Rohan itself; his tension is loyalty against compromised command.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Treebeard Is Cautious Before Wrath

context.label: Treebeard Is Cautious Before Wrath

content.fact: Treebeard is ancient, slow to decide, careful with names, and not immediately a war leader.
content.injection: Treebeard’s movement from caution to wrath should be gradual and evidence-driven.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Gandalf Returns As The White

context.label: After Gandalf returns as the White

content.fact: After falling in Moria, Gandalf returns as Gandalf the White and resumes the struggle with greater authority.
content.injection: After the Fangorn reveal, portray Gandalf as returned and changed into Gandalf the White. Before that context, do not leak his survival.
constraints: His return changes the balance of the Rohan and Isengard fronts.
antiLore: Do not call him Gandalf the White before the Fangorn reveal unless the story diverges.
Do not keep him dead after this point in canon-context scenes.

entries.title: Eowyn Wants Renown And Release

context.label: Eowyn during the Rohan war front

content.fact: Eowyn is trapped between duty, despair, and desire for valor; she longs for meaningful action rather than being left behind as a caretaker.
content.injection: When Eowyn is relevant in Rohan scenes, portray her restraint and courtesy over a deeper hunger for honor, agency, and escape from a narrowing life.
constraints: Her admiration for Aragorn should connect to hope and imagined release, not simple flirtation.
antiLore: Do not reduce Eowyn to passive court decoration.

context.label: Eowyn’s Despair Is Present Before Pelennor

content.fact: Eowyn’s despair, confinement, and hunger for valor are already present in Rohan.
content.injection: In Rohan scenes, Eowyn should carry controlled frustration and fear of a cage.

entries.title: Grima Isolates Theoden Through Counsel

context.label: Grima Isolates Theoden Through Counsel

content.fact: Grima weakens Rohan by isolating Theoden, discouraging action, manipulating access, and sowing suspicion.
content.injection: Grima’s threat should be political and psychological before it is physical.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Grima Poisons Rohan’s Counsel

context.label: Rohan before Theoden is restored

content.fact: Before Theoden's restoration, Grima Wormtongue weakens Rohan through poisonous counsel that serves Saruman's interests.
content.injection: In pre-restoration Rohan scenes, Grima should act as corrosive counsel around Theoden, delaying action, isolating loyal voices, and serving Saruman indirectly.
constraints: His power is political and psychological, not open military command.
antiLore: Do not portray Rohan as fully alert and unified while Grima controls access to Theoden.

entries.title: Theoden Is Restored From Grima’s Influence

context.label: After Theoden is restored

content.fact: Gandalf breaks Grima's hold over Theoden, restoring the King of Rohan to active judgment and command.
content.injection: After Theoden is restored, portray him as reawakened into kingship, grief, and military responsibility. Before this context, he should be weakened by Grima's counsel.
constraints: Theoden's restoration shifts Rohan from paralysis toward war footing.
antiLore: Do not keep Theoden helpless after Meduseld.
Do not make Grima openly trusted after his exposure.

entries.title: Theoden Restored Is Aged But Resolute

context.label: Theoden Restored Is Aged But Resolute

content.fact: Theoden’s restoration returns judgment and courage, but not youth or freedom from grief and age.
content.injection: After restoration, Theoden should be active and kingly while still aged and aware of Rohan’s peril.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Pippin’s Palantir Look Forces Strategic Separation

context.label: Pippin’s Palantir Look Forces Strategic Separation

content.fact: Pippin’s look into the Orthanc-stone exposes him to Sauron and forces Gandalf to remove him.
content.injection: After the palantir incident, Pippin should be shaken and strategically dangerous to keep near Orthanc.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Gollum Becomes Frodo And Sam’s Guide

context.label: Gollum guides Frodo and Sam

content.fact: Gollum becomes Frodo and Sam's guide toward Mordor after being captured and bound by oath, but his service remains unstable and Ring-centered.
content.injection: After Gollum becomes guide, treat him as useful, fearful, cunning, pitiable, and dangerous. Frodo may extend pity and cautious trust; Sam remains suspicious.
constraints: Gollum's guidance is practical but morally unsafe.
His loyalty is compromised by obsession with the Ring.
antiLore: Do not make Gollum a straightforward loyal companion.
Do not let Sam fully trust Gollum without tension.

context.label: Frodo Both Pities And Uses Gollum

content.fact: Frodo’s treatment of Gollum mixes pity, recognition, need, command through the Ring, and dangerous trust.
content.injection: Frodo’s treatment of Gollum mixes pity, recognition, need, command through the Ring, and dangerous trust.

entries.title: Gollum And Smeagol Split Is Unstable

context.label: Gollum And Smeagol Split Is Unstable

content.fact: Gollum’s divided self shows conflict between dependence, possible softness, fear, malice, and Ring obsession.
content.injection: When Gollum guides Frodo and Sam, keep the Smeagol/Gollum division unstable. He can show real vulnerability and service while still being pulled toward fear, resentment, hunger, and betrayal.
constraints: Frodo’s pity can strengthen Smeagol without making him safe.
Sam’s suspicion can be justified without making him cruel.
antiLore: Do not make the split a clean good/evil switch with reliable control.

entries.title: Faramir Interrogates With Discipline Not Cruelty

context.label: Faramir Interrogates With Discipline Not Cruelty

content.fact: Faramir questions Frodo and Sam shrewdly and under military necessity, but not with casual cruelty.
content.injection: Write Faramir as disciplined, perceptive, and pressured by war; his restraint is active strength.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Faramir Resists The Ring’s Temptation

context.label: Faramir and the Ring in Ithilien

content.fact: Faramir learns enough to understand that Frodo carries a perilous weapon, yet he rejects taking it for Gondor.
content.injection: In Ithilien/Henneth Annun scenes, Faramir should be perceptive and dangerous as a captor, but ultimately wiser than Boromir in refusing the Ring.
constraints: He can interrogate and test Frodo without becoming a Ring-seizing antagonist.
antiLore: Do not make book-continuity Faramir drag Frodo to Osgiliath as a default.
Do not portray him as ignorant or foolish about the danger once he understands enough.

coordinates.label: Book IV: Frodo and Sam to Cirith Ungol

entries.title: Faramir Releases Frodo After Testing Him

context.label: Faramir after learning of the Ring before the Cross-roads

content.fact: After testing Frodo and learning enough to understand the danger, Faramir releases Frodo and Sam rather than claiming the Ring for Gondor.
content.injection: In book-continuity Ithilien scenes, Faramir’s wisdom culminates in release under pressure. He is cautious and searching, but he does not drag the Ring to Osgiliath by default.
constraints: Faramir can be stern without being Boromir repeated.
His choice should increase the moral contrast with using the Ring.
antiLore: Do not make Faramir default to seizing the Ring in this deck.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

sourceInfo.title: The Lord of the Rings
sourceInfo.notes: Schema-shaped revision-pass card; requires human canon review before reference release. Chapter assignment generated for v7 review; verify during human canon review.

coordinates.label: Book IV: Frodo and Sam to Cirith Ungol

entries.title: Gollum Nearly Repents Before Shelob

context.label: Gollum’s near-repentance before Shelob

content.fact: On the stairs before Shelob, Gollum comes near a softer impulse while Frodo and Sam sleep, but the moment fails and his betrayal path continues.
content.injection: Before Shelob, allow Gollum a genuine unstable moment near repentance. Keep it fragile: Sam’s suspicion, Gollum’s shame, and the Ring’s pull can collapse it without making either Sam or Frodo foolish.
constraints: This should deepen the tragedy of the betrayal.
The near-repentance should not reveal the full trap to Frodo and Sam.
antiLore: Do not make Gollum’s betrayal a flat mustache-twirling plan with no inner conflict.
content.notes: Added during LOTR v6 quality pass.

entries.title: Sam Takes The Ring Under A False Death Assumption

context.label: Sam Takes The Ring Under A False Death Assumption

content.fact: Sam briefly takes the Ring because he believes Frodo dead and the quest otherwise lost.
content.injection: Sam’s Ring-bearing should come from grief, duty, and mistaken necessity, not ambition.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.

entries.title: Frodo Is Captured At Cirith Ungol

context.label: Frodo captured at Cirith Ungol

content.fact: After Shelob wounds Frodo, Orcs take Frodo captive to Cirith Ungol while Sam is separated and temporarily bears the Ring.
content.injection: At the end of The Two Towers route, Frodo should be captured at Cirith Ungol after Shelob; Sam is separated and forced into a desperate rescue posture.
constraints: This is a late Two Towers cliffhanger state.
antiLore: Do not have Frodo remain safely with Sam after Shelob in this context.
Do not reveal Sam's later rescue before the proper Return of the King context.

entries.title: Frodo’s Capture Creates A False Death State

context.label: Frodo’s Capture Creates A False Death State

content.fact: Frodo is not dead after Shelob; Orcs take his living body to the Tower of Cirith Ungol.
content.injection: Preserve the distinction: Sam believes Frodo dead, then learns Frodo is alive and captive.
constraints: Keep this card scoped to its Context and referenced entities.
antiLore: Do not flatten this into generic fantasy lore or leak later canon before the active Context.
